cataloging_subject.label: The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS. (edited selection: 95 poems in 93 editorial units)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The electronic selection delimited by the pinned U-M XML: two printed parts issued in 1892 and 1901, containing 95 intellectual poems arranged as 93 numbered/count-once editorial units, plus wrappers, parallel comparison streams, sidenotes, Various Readings, and editorial apparatus. It is not one medieval work, an authorial collection, the entire Vernon manuscript, or a claim that every reading is Vernon based.

catalog_summary.title: The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS. (edited selection: 95 poems in 93 editorial units)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous poets, adapters, compilers, and translators, with disputed or traditional part-level attributions
catalog_summary.editor: Carl Horstmann (1892 Part I) and Frederick J. Furnivall (1901 Part II)
catalog_summary.date: No single medieval composition date assigned; edited parts published 1892 and 1901; electronic layer published 2006
catalog_summary.region: Middle English cultural and linguistic context; no single composition place established for all 95 poems

summaries.value: An electronic edited selection of 95 Middle English verse poems arranged as 93 editorial units in Carl Horstmann's 1892 Part I and Frederick J. Furnivall's 1901 Part II. Vernon is the principal but not exclusive witness: the editions also print parallel witness texts, supplied passages, earlier-edition readings, collations, sidenotes, Various Readings, and an Appendix. The collection ranges across prayer, liturgical and devotional verse, moral instruction, narrative, debate, satire, and historical polemic.

Electronic edited selection of 95 intellectual poems arranged as 93 numbered/count-once editorial units. Item XL contains two explicit complete poems, and Item XXXVIII embeds the distinct complete 1,247-line Myrour adaptation. The hierarchy has 93 count-bearing editorial units, while item_count and structured extent use the 95-intellectual-poem axis: Items XXXVIII and XL are aggregate parents with item_count=2, and their three overlapping detail children remain null to prevent double counting.

extent.description: 95 intellectual poems in 93 numbered/count-once editorial units and 101 modeled parts, printed across pages 1–786; aggregate line, stanza, and word totals remain null because the XML serializes parallel witnesses, collations, quoted comparisons, prose notes, and repeated page references.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Direct XML intellectual-unit audit
evidence.summary: The retained boundary contains 95 intellectual poems. Aggregate Item XXXVIII counts Castel and the distinct complete 1,247-line Myrour adaptation; aggregate Item XL counts its two complete poems. Their overlapping detail children remain null so the item_count sum does not double count them.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XIX comparison heading
evidence.summary: Comparison copied as given by Morris 1871, whose source is current Liturg. 104.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item II argument
evidence.summary: Adler/Kaluza print mediation and Digby 18 base; Rawlinson and Ashmole collations.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXX argument and streams
evidence.summary: Vernon, CUL Dd.1.1, and Harley 4196 are full printed streams. Simeon appears in exactly two apparatus notes, not a transcribed stream.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Reproducible Item XXXI apparatus count
evidence.summary: There are 173 NOTE1 strings whose normalized text begins 'F.', plus 21 additional F.-bearing omission or cross-reference notes, versus two NOTE1 strings containing 'Simeon'. The review count of 174 F.-readings is not reproduced and remains an explicit reconciliation discrepancy.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXXI notes
evidence.summary: The main Vernon stream carries dominant Furnivall 1862 comparison apparatus; Simeon appears in exactly two notes.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXXIV contextual-edition audit
evidence.summary: The heading cites Kaufmann 1889 and a Turnbull 1843/NLS/CUL different-version branch as context, while the actual complete Cotton stream explicitly names Furnivall 1866. Those contextual citations are not asserted as sources of encoded readings.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXXIV stream and apparatus audit
evidence.summary: Horstmann 1892 prints the first Vernon occurrence as the main text, collates it through 41 explicit V2 notes from the second Vernon occurrence, and copies a separate complete Cotton Caligula A II stream through Furnivall 1866; all remain one count-once item.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXXVI argument and source method
evidence.summary: Horstmann prints Vernon as base and explicitly supplies various readings from CUL Dd.1.1, Trinity College Oxford MS 57, and unresolved MS. L. 70; he attributes the poem to Richard Maidstone and explicitly rejects Richard Rolle.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XXXVIII comparison heading
evidence.summary: Halliwell 1849 intermediary and unidentified private manuscript base.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XL heading and notes
evidence.summary: Item XL explicitly contains two complete poems (60 and 384 L elements). Its aggregate parent has item_count=2, while the two overlapping analytical children remain null to prevent double counting.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XLV collation responsibility
evidence.summary: The edition states that Miss A. F. Parker collates the text with the manuscript from this point onward.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XLVII heading
evidence.summary: Simmons 1879 sidenotes reprinted while main text remains Vernon based.

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item XLVIII streams

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item L source notes

evidence.citation: APE7335 lineage manifest evidence: Item LIV streams

open_questions.question: What is the present identity, repository, and access status of Halliwell's private manuscript source for The Castel of Love?
next_steps: Trace Halliwell's ownership and citation history in later manuscript scholarship and repository accession records.

open_questions.question: What is the present identity, repository, and access status of the Burton leaf used for Appendix no. 9?
next_steps: Trace Furnivall's Burton-leaf reference through later scholarship and institutional accession records.

open_questions.question: Where exactly on Digby 2 f. 6r does Appendix no. 1 begin?
next_steps: Obtain and inspect authorized images of Digby 2 f. 6r-v and map the edition's opening against the leaf.

open_questions.question: Did Horstmann transcribe Item II's supplied opening directly from Digby 18 or copy it through Adler and Kaluza's 1887 edition?
next_steps: Compare the supplied 22 lines and variants against the Digby 18 images and Adler-Kaluza print before upgrading the supported print-mediated route.

open_questions.question: How should the current fifteenth-century date for Egerton 927 be reconciled with Horstmann's later-fourteenth-century description?
next_steps: Seek current codicological scholarship or repository clarification; retain both source-attributed dates until resolved.

open_questions.question: How should historical and current Vernon foliation be reconciled, including the exact locus of Item XL's two songs?
next_steps: Build a foliation concordance from the complete provider inventory and seek repository confirmation for disputed boundaries.

open_questions.question: Where exactly does Appendix no. 3 begin within the Digby 86 target run?
next_steps: Collate the edition opening against the cached Digby 86 images and obtain repository clarification if the seam remains ambiguous.

open_questions.question: How should Dd.1.1's conflicting dates, missing folio, and Item XXX's 38-line opening absent from Vernon be reconciled?
next_steps: Obtain current manuscript images or repository clarification and preserve the 1856 and 1885 date claims independently.

open_questions.question: How do Furnivall's f. 84v col. 2, Horstmann's f. 86, and the current BL ff. 86v-88r loci for the Cotton Trentale correspond?
next_steps: Obtain current Cotton Caligula A II images and build a text-to-foliation concordance without silently harmonizing the three statements.

open_questions.question: What U-M production steps beyond the recovered package and TEI header led from TIFFs and raw XML to the published encoding?
next_steps: Seek U-M production documentation while retaining only the package-supported encoding, TIFF, and normalized-copy relations.

open_questions.question: What reuse terms govern the cached Bodleian GitHub TEI catalog prose?
next_steps: Locate a resource-level license or obtain written permission before redistributing the cached catalog files.

notes: Count 95 intellectual poems through 93 count-bearing editorial parents: Items XXXVIII and XL contribute two each, and their three overlapping analytical children remain null.
Keep prose arguments, notes, sidenotes, and apparatus as editorial layers; they do not make the selected poems mixed-form or make modern English a work language.
Scope Latin and Anglo-Norman only to the exact retained parts and passages recorded in the language statements; rubric-only language is excluded.
Preserve the duplicated Roman number XLV through stable part IDs rather than silently renumbering the edition.
Keep manuscript, edition, encoding, facsimile, package, access, and rights facts on their own layers in the linked lineage manifest.
